message.content:
You are translating a React SPA (CitedBy.ai) to support i18n (FR/EN/ES). Your job is to replace ALL hardcoded French text in the following 5 pages with i18n translation keys.

PROJECT ROOT: C:/Users/User/citedby-ai

PATTERN TO FOLLOW:
1. At the top of each page file, add: `import { useTranslation } from '../i18n/context';`
2. Inside the component function, add: `const { t } = useTranslation();`
3. Replace every hardcoded French string with `t.sectionName.keyName`
4. For data arrays with French text, move them INSIDE the component so they can reference `t.*`
5. Fill the corresponding empty section in the 3 locale files with actual translations

FILES TO MODIFY:
- pages/Login.tsx → section `login` in locale files
- pages/Account.tsx → section `account` in locale files
- pages/Plan.tsx → section `plan` in locale files
- pages/Recharge.tsx → section `rechargeCredits` in locale files
- pages/History.tsx → section `history` in locale files

LOCALE FILES (all at C:/Users/User/citedby-ai/i18n/locales/):
- fr.ts: Replace `login: {},` with `login: { title: 'Connexion', ... },` etc. (French = original text)
- en.ts: Replace `login: {},` with `login: { title: 'Login', ... },` etc. (English translations)
- es.ts: Replace `login: {},` with `login: { title: 'Iniciar sesión', ... },` etc. (Spanish translations)

IMPORTANT RULES:
- Do NOT modify any files in services/, app/, lib/, supabase/
- Replace ALL visible French text (headings, labels, buttons, placeholders, descriptions, tab names, tooltips, error messages, empty states)
- Use the existing `common` section keys (t.common.back, t.common.save, etc.) for shared terms
- Use the existing `nav` section for navigation labels
- Keep ALL existing functionality intact - only change the text rendering
- For template strings with variables, use a pattern like: `t.section.key` and handle interpolation

Read each page first, then make all changes. Work through all 5 pages systematically.
